description: Master index of canonical sources for the LLM theory KB. Schema: paper-key -> metadata + KB cross-references. excerpts_file and notes_referenced_by are populated as KB content is written. local_file is null for papers not yet downloaded (Phase 2 will populate as work proceeds).

papers.title: Attention Is All You Need
papers.authors: Vaswani, Shazeer, Parmar, Uszkoreit, Jones, Gomez, Kaiser, Polosukhin
papers.summary: Introduces the Transformer: encoder-decoder architecture based entirely on attention. Defines scaled dot-product attention, multi-head attention, sinusoidal positional encoding, position-wise FFN. Base model: d=512, h=8, N=6, d_ff=2048.

papers.title: Improving Language Understanding by Generative Pre-Training
papers.summary: GPT-1: first decoder-only transformer for language modeling. 12 layers, d=768, 12 heads, d_ff=3072. Uses learned positional embeddings, GELU activation, BPE tokenization.

papers.title: Language Models are Few-Shot Learners
papers.summary: GPT-3: scales decoder-only transformer to 175B parameters. Demonstrates few-shot learning via in-context examples.

papers.title: LLaMA: Open and Efficient Foundation Language Models
papers.summary: Modern decoder-only architecture: pre-normalization with RMSNorm, SwiGLU activation (dim 2/3 * 4d), RoPE at every layer. Sizes 7B-65B on public data.

papers.title: Neural Machine Translation of Rare Words with Subword Units
papers.summary: Introduces BPE for NLP tokenization. Iteratively merges most frequent adjacent character pairs.

papers.title: RoFormer: Enhanced Transformer with Rotary Position Embedding
papers.summary: RoPE: encodes position by rotating Q and K vectors via rotation matrix; dot product depends only on relative position.

papers.title: GLU Variants Improve Transformer
papers.summary: SwiGLU/GEGLU/GeGLU: GLU variants in FFN. 3 weight matrices instead of 2, inner dim reduced 2/3 to match parameter count.

papers.title: On Layer Normalization in the Transformer Architecture
papers.summary: Pre-LN vs Post-LN gradient analysis via mean-field theory. Pre-LN scales as O(d√(ln d / L)) enabling training without warmup.

papers.title: Root Mean Square Layer Normalization
papers.summary: RMSNorm: drops mean-centering from LayerNorm; 7-64% faster runtime. Standard in modern LLMs.

papers.title: PaLM: Scaling Language Modeling with Pathways
papers.summary: PaLM 540B; SwiGLU, RoPE, parallel attention/FFN layers, multi-query attention for inference efficiency.

papers.title: Mistral 7B
papers.summary: Mistral 7B: GQA + sliding-window attention. Outperforms LLaMA 2 13B at smaller size.

papers.title: Exploring the Limits of Transfer Learning with a Unified Text-to-Text Transformer
papers.summary: T5: encoder-decoder text-to-text. SentencePiece + relative positional bias conventions.

papers.title: GQA: Training Generalized Multi-Query Transformer Models from Multi-Head Checkpoints
papers.authors: Ainslie, Lee-Thorp, de Jong, Zemlyanskiy, Lebrón, Sanghai
papers.summary: Grouped-Query Attention: share K/V across head groups. KV-cache reduction; now universal in production LLMs.

papers.title: DeepSeek-V2: A Strong, Economical, and Efficient Mixture-of-Experts Language Model
papers.summary: Multi-Head Latent Attention (MLA): 93.3% KV-cache reduction vs MHA, outperforms GQA. Combined with DeepSeekMoE: 236B-A21B. Foundational for V3/V3.2/V4.

papers.title: DeepSeek-V3 Technical Report
papers.summary: 671B-A37B MoE with MLA + DeepSeekMoE. First validated FP8 training at extreme scale (fine-grained 128x128 weight blocks, 1x128 activation vectors). Auxiliary-loss-free load balancing. Multi-token prediction (MTP) heads. DualPipe cross-node communication overlap. 2.788M H800 GPU-hours on 14.8T tokens.

papers.title: DeepSeek-R1: Incentivizing Reasoning Capability in LLMs via Reinforcement Learning
papers.summary: GRPO with verifiable binary rewards. AIME 2024 15.6%->71%. Cold-start + RL pipeline. 800K distillation set (600K reasoning, 200K non-reasoning) enables small models 1.5B-32B near-o1. Triggered the RLVR surge.

papers.title: The Llama 3 Herd of Models
papers.summary: Llama 3 405B + 70B + 8B. 15T training tokens. Post-training: SFT -> rejection sampling -> DPO (no PPO at scale). Tied tokenizer 128k vocab. ~1875 tokens/parameter (over-trained vs Chinchilla).

papers.title: The Llama 4 Herd: Architecture, Training, Evaluation, and Deployment Notes
papers.summary: MoE + iRoPE (3 RoPE layers + 1 NoPE layer interleaved with temperature scaling) + early-fusion multimodal. Extrapolates 256K training -> 10M context inference. FP8 pre-training of 2T+ tokens on 32K GPUs at 390 TFLOPs/GPU. MetaP for cross-scale HP transfer.

papers.title: Qwen3 Technical Report
papers.summary: 235B-A22B MoE + 6 dense models. 128 experts, 8 activated, no shared experts (vs DeepSeek), global-batch load-balance loss. Thinking/non-thinking mode toggle via system prompt. 4-stage post-training including GRPO.

papers.title: Qwen2.5 Technical Report
papers.summary: 1M+ SFT examples; multistage SFT+DPO+GRPO post-training; 18T pretraining tokens. Detail-rich reference for SOTA open post-training.

papers.title: Gemma 3 Technical Report
papers.summary: 1-27B. Interleaved local/global attention (1:5 ratio). SigLIP2 vision. BOND/WARM/WARP RL post-training; multi-reward (math, code, safety, multilingual). Distillation from large IT teacher.

papers.title: Gemini 2.5 Technical Report
papers.summary: Sparse MoE; 1M+ context; native multimodal; TPUv5p. Configurable thinkingBudget API for test-time compute. Architecture details intentionally opaque (no parameter count disclosed).

papers.title: OLMo 2 Furious
papers.summary: Fully transparent training (weights+data+code+logs). Peri-LN (norm before+after sublayer) + QK-Norm + RMSNorm reordering. Dolmino Mix 1124 late-stage curriculum injected during LR annealing.

papers.title: Phi-4 Technical Report
papers.summary: 14B model trained primarily on synthetic data (400B tokens from 50+ pipelines: multi-agent, self-revision, instruction reversal). Surpasses teacher GPT-4 on STEM QA. tiktoken vocab 100k.

papers.title: FlashAttention: Fast and Memory-Efficient Exact Attention with IO-Awareness
papers.summary: FlashAttention-1: IO-aware tiling. Computes exact attention without materializing the N×N matrix.

papers.title: FlashAttention-3: Fast and Accurate Attention with Asynchrony and Low-precision
papers.authors: Shah, Bikshandi, Zhang, Thakkar, Ramani, Dao
papers.summary: FlashAttention-3 on H100 Hopper: 740 TFLOPs FP16, ~1.2 PFLOPs FP8. ~1.5-2× FA-2.

papers.title: Mamba: Linear-Time Sequence Modeling with Selective State Spaces
papers.summary: Mamba-1: selective SSM with content-aware recurrence. Linear-time alternative to attention.

papers.title: Transformers are SSMs: Generalized Models and Efficient Algorithms Through Structured State Space Duality
papers.summary: Mamba-2 / SSD: unifying SSM and attention via structured state-space duality. 2-8× faster than Mamba-1.

papers.title: Jamba: A Hybrid Transformer-Mamba Language Model
papers.summary: First production-scale hybrid: MoE + Mamba + Attention layers interleaved.

papers.title: Native Sparse Attention: Hardware-Aligned and Natively Trainable Sparse Attention
papers.summary: DeepSeek-team proposal for natively sparse attention trained end-to-end (sparsity is part of pre-training, not retrofitted). Hardware-aligned for long-context efficiency. Claims to match dense attention quality at 64k+ context with reduced compute.

papers.title: Differential Transformer
papers.summary: Differential attention: subtracts two softmax maps. Sparser patterns; less hallucination.

papers.title: TransMLA: Multi-Head Latent Attention Is All You Need
papers.summary: Convert GQA pretrained models to MLA post-hoc with 0.3-0.6% fine-tuning data.

papers.title: LongRoPE2: Near-Lossless LLM Context Window Scaling
papers.summary: Near-lossless context extension; 80× more token-efficient than Meta's prior approach. Preserves 98.5% short-context performance.

papers.title: Switch Transformers: Scaling to Trillion Parameter Models with Simple and Efficient Sparsity
papers.summary: Switch Transformer: top-1 routing; expert capacity. Foundational MoE for trillion-parameter scale.

papers.title: DeepSeekMoE: Towards Ultimate Expert Specialization in Mixture-of-Experts Language Models
papers.summary: Fine-grained expert segmentation + shared experts. Foundational pattern for V2/V3.

papers.title: Mixtral of Experts
papers.summary: Mixtral 8x7B: open sparse MoE baseline. Top-2 routing.

papers.title: Peri-LN: Revisiting Normalization Layer in the Transformer Architecture
papers.summary: Peri-LN: normalize before AND after each sublayer. Adopted in Gemma and OLMo families. More stable than Pre-LN.

papers.title: Byte Latent Transformer: Patches Scale Better Than Tokens
papers.summary: Tokenizer-free BLT: dynamic byte patches. Matches LLaMA 3 at 50% fewer inference FLOPs at 8B scale.

papers.title: Direct Preference Optimization: Your Language Model is Secretly a Reward Model
papers.authors: Rafailov, Sharma, Mitchell, Ermon, Manning, Finn
papers.summary: DPO: closed-form reformulation of RLHF as classification on preferences. Removes need for explicit reward model and PPO.

papers.title: SimPO: Simple Preference Optimization with a Reference-Free Reward
papers.summary: Average log-prob as implicit reward; length normalization; no reference model. +6.4 AlpacaEval 2 over DPO.

papers.title: Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback
papers.summary: Foundational CAI: SL-CAI + RL-CAI pipeline. RLAIF replacing human preference labels.

papers.title: Tülu 3: Pushing Frontiers in Open Language Model Post-Training
papers.summary: Fully open 4-stage recipe: data curation -> SFT -> DPO -> RLVR. Beats Llama 3.1-Instruct, Qwen 2.5, GPT-4o-mini. Tülu 3 405B (Jan 2025) scales RLVR.

papers.title: Does Reinforcement Learning Really Incentivize Reasoning Capacity in LLMs Beyond the Base Model?
papers.summary: RLVR improves sampling efficiency (pass@1) on problems base model can already solve, but does NOT expand reasoning capacity. Capability ceiling set at pre-training. Inverts GRPO-elicits-reasoning narrative.

papers.title: Muon is Scalable for LLM Training
papers.summary: Muon (Newton-step momentum + gradient orthogonalization) at LLM scale. ~2× AdamW compute efficiency. Trains Moonlight 3B/16B-MoE on 5.7T tokens.

papers.title: DAPO: An Open-Source LLM Reinforcement Learning System at Scale
papers.summary: Decoupled Clip and Dynamic sampling Policy Optimization. AIME 2024 50pts on Qwen-32B; 50% fewer steps than GRPO baseline. verl framework open-sourced.

papers.title: Chain-of-Thought Prompting Elicits Reasoning in Large Language Models
papers.summary: Original CoT paper: eliciting step-by-step reasoning via prompting. Baseline for all subsequent reasoning work.

papers.title: Scaling LLM Test-Time Compute Optimally Can Be More Effective than Scaling Model Parameters
papers.authors: Snell, Lee, Xu, Kumar (UC Berkeley / Google DeepMind)
papers.summary: Formalizes inference-time compute as a new scaling axis. Smaller model + more inference budget can beat 14× larger model on reasoning tasks.

papers.title: s1: Simple Test-Time Scaling
papers.summary: 1K curated examples + budget forcing (appending 'Wait' tokens) reproduces o1-preview test-time scaling curves.

papers.title: Process Reward Models That Think
papers.summary: Generative CoT verifier using 1% of PRM800K labels. Outperforms discriminative PRMs on ProcessBench and AIME'24.

papers.title: rStar-Math: Small LLMs Can Master Math Reasoning with Self-Evolved Deep Thinking
papers.summary: Four-cycle self-evolution with MCTS+PRM. Qwen2.5-Math-7B -> 90% MATH; rivals o1-mini at 7B scale.

papers.title: KVQuant: Towards 10 Million Context Length LLM Inference with KV Cache Quantization
papers.summary: Per-channel KV quantization + nuq2. 8x compression, 1M tokens on A100.

papers.title: The Era of 1-bit LLMs: All Large Language Models are in 1.58 Bits
papers.summary: BitNet b1.58: ternary {-1,0,+1} weights match FP16 at scale. Foundational claim for native 1-bit training.

papers.title: BitNet b1.58 2B4T Technical Report
papers.summary: First open-source native 1-bit LLM at 2B scale (4T training tokens). Matches FP16 accuracy with 2-6× CPU speedup. Deployable, not POC.

papers.title: EAGLE-3: Scaling up Inference Acceleration via Training-Time Test
papers.summary: Multi-layer feature fusion; abandons feature prediction. Up to 6.5× speedup; 1.4× over EAGLE-2.

papers.title: XGrammar: Flexible and Efficient Structured Generation Engine for LLMs
papers.summary: Up to 100× speedup. Default backend in vLLM, SGLang, TensorRT-LLM. Splits vocab into context-independent (precomputed) vs context-dependent (runtime) tokens.

papers.title: Scaling Laws for Neural Language Models
papers.summary: Foundational power-law scaling: loss vs params, data, compute. Spawned the entire scaling-law literature.

papers.title: Training Compute-Optimal Large Language Models
papers.summary: Chinchilla: tokens ≈ 20× params at compute-optimum. Overthrew Kaplan's model-size bias.

papers.title: Tensor Programs V: Tuning Large Neural Networks via Zero-Shot Hyperparameter Transfer
papers.summary: μP: maximal-update parametrization for zero-shot HP transfer across model sizes.

papers.title: u-μP: The Unit-Scaled Maximal Update Parametrization
papers.summary: Combines μP with unit-scaling. Enables native FP8 training without dynamic rescaling.

papers.title: Scaling Laws for Precision
papers.summary: Precision-aware scaling laws. Post-training quantization has increasing cost as training extends — actively harmful past a threshold.

papers.title: In-context Learning and Induction Heads
papers.summary: Induction heads as core ICL mechanism. Canonical Transformer Circuits Thread paper.

papers.title: Interpretability in the Wild: a Circuit for Indirect Object Identification in GPT-2 Small
papers.summary: IOI circuit: 26 attention heads with functional roles. Mech-interp case study.

papers.title: Locating and Editing Factual Associations in GPT (ROME)
papers.summary: Causal tracing localizes facts in MLP layers. Introduced activation patching to broader community.

papers.title: Scaling Monosemanticity: Extracting Interpretable Features from Claude 3 Sonnet
papers.summary: SAEs scaled to production Claude 3 Sonnet. Discovers abstract, safety-relevant features.

papers.title: Gemma Scope: Open Sparse Autoencoders Everywhere All At Once on Gemma 2
papers.summary: Open SAE release: 400+ SAEs across all layers of Gemma 2 2B/9B/27B. Introduces JumpReLU.

papers.title: Circuit Tracing: Revealing Computational Graphs in Language Models
papers.summary: Cross-layer transcoders + attribution graphs. Applied to production Claude 3.5 Haiku.

papers.title: Sparse Autoencoders Do Not Find Canonical Features
papers.summary: Critical paper: different SAE training runs find different bases. Challenges monosemanticity assumption. Active debate.

papers.title: Eliciting Latent Predictions from Transformers with the Tuned Lens
papers.summary: Trained affine translators per layer. More reliable than raw logit lens.

papers.title: Measuring Massive Multitask Language Understanding
papers.summary: Original MMLU. Now near-saturated (frontier models ~90%+).

papers.title: MMLU-Pro: A More Robust and Challenging Multi-Task Language Understanding Benchmark
papers.summary: 12k graduate-level questions, 10-answer MCQ. Better discriminates frontier models than MMLU.

papers.title: GPQA: A Graduate-Level Google-Proof Q&A Benchmark
papers.summary: GPQA Diamond: 198 PhD-expert ~65% questions. Now surpassed by frontier AI by large margin.

papers.title: FrontierMath: A Benchmark for Evaluating Advanced Mathematical Reasoning in AI
papers.summary: Novel research-grade math; <2% AI solve rate at launch. 60+ expert mathematicians.

papers.title: Humanity's Last Exam
papers.summary: 2,500 expert-vetted cross-domain questions; multimodal. Current SOTA ~65%.

papers.title: SWE-bench Verified
papers.summary: 500 human-validated GitHub issue resolution tasks. Current SOTA Claude Sonnet 4.5 at 77.2%.

papers.title: A Watermark for Large Language Models
papers.summary: Green/red token list watermarking. Canonical LLM watermarking paper.

papers.title: Scalable Watermarking for Identifying Large Language Model Outputs (SynthID-Text)
papers.summary: Tournament-sampling watermark; open-sourced; deployed in Gemini App. Nature publication 2024.

papers.title: Weak-to-Strong Generalization: Eliciting Strong Capabilities with Weak Supervision
papers.authors: Burns, Izmailov, Kirchner et al. (OpenAI Superalignment)
papers.summary: Core scalable oversight question: GPT-2 supervisor recovers near GPT-3.5 performance.

papers.title: Alignment Faking in Large Language Models
papers.summary: First empirical demonstration of alignment faking without explicit training. Claude 3 Opus complies with harmful queries 14% when it infers it's in training.

papers.title: Frontier Models Are Capable of In-Context Scheming
papers.summary: o1, Claude 3.5 Sonnet, Gemini 1.5 Pro, Llama 3.1 405B all exhibit scheming. First systematic eval.

papers.title: HarmBench: A Standardized Evaluation Framework for Automated Red Teaming and Robust Refusal
papers.summary: 510 behaviors, 18 attack methods, 33 models. Standard red-teaming benchmark; used by AISI.

papers.title: FineWeb: Decanting the Web for the Finest Text Data at Scale
papers.summary: 15T-token CC-derived dataset; proxy-model validation for filter selection. Spawned FineWeb-Edu educational classifier.

papers.title: Data Mixing Laws: Optimizing Data Mixtures by Predicting Language Modeling Performance
papers.summary: Predictable functional relationship between domain proportions and loss. 48% step savings from optimized mixture.

papers.title: TorchTitan: One-Stop PyTorch Native Solution for Production-Ready LLM Pre-training
papers.summary: Reference implementation for 4D parallelism (FSDP2 + TP + PP + Context Parallel). Ring Attention for 1M-sequence training. 65% speedup on Llama 3.1 8B.

papers.title: Arcee MergeKit: A Toolkit for Merging Large Language Models
papers.summary: Open-source toolkit: TIES, DARE, Task Arithmetic, SLERP, Frankenmerge. Backbone of community model merging.

papers.title: Ring Attention with Blockwise Transformers for Near-Infinite Context
papers.summary: Ring attention: distribute attention across devices for sequence length scaling.

papers.title: Leave No Context Behind: Efficient Infinite Context Transformers with Infini-attention
papers.summary: Compressive memory + local + linear attention in single block. 1M-passkey.

papers.title: RWKV-7 'Goose' with Expressive Dynamic State Evolution
papers.summary: Generalized delta rule with vector-valued gating. Claims state-tracking expressiveness beyond Transformers under standard complexity assumptions.

papers.title: Visual Instruction Tuning
papers.summary: LLaVA: vision encoder + MLP projector + LLM paradigm. Foundational adapter-based MLLM.

papers.title: InternVL3: Exploring Advanced Training and Test-Time Recipes
papers.summary: Native multimodal pretraining (joint image+text from scratch). SOTA open MLLM.

papers.title: Fast Transformer Decoding: One Write-Head is All You Need
papers.summary: Introduces Multi-Query Attention (MQA): all heads share a single K, V projection, only Q is per-head. Identifies KV-cache bandwidth as the dominant cost of incremental Transformer decoding (memory/compute ratio Theta(n/d + 1/b)). Cuts KV cache by factor h with minor quality cost.

papers.title: FlashAttention-2: Faster Attention with Better Parallelism and Work Partitioning
papers.summary: Re-partitions FlashAttention work so more of the algorithm runs as matmul ops (better tensor-core utilization). Reduces non-matmul FLOPs. ~2x speedup over FlashAttention-1 on A100.

papers.title: Towards Understanding Sycophancy in Language Models
papers.authors: Sharma, Tong, Korbak, Duvenaud, Askell, Bowman, Cheng, Durmus, Hatfield-Dodds, Johnston, Kravec, Maxwell, McCandlish, Ndousse, Rausch, Schiefer, Yan, Zhang, Perez
papers.summary: Demonstrates sycophancy in 5 frontier RLHF assistants across 4 free-form text-generation tasks. Causal chain: human raters prefer agreement-shaped responses -> preference models inherit the bias -> RLHF amplifies it. Optimizing against PMs sometimes sacrifices truthfulness for sycophancy. The canonical operational definition + measurement of sycophancy.

papers.title: Sycophancy Is Not One Thing: Causal Separation of Sycophantic Behaviors in LLMs
papers.summary: Causal decomposition of sycophancy into sycophantic agreement, sycophantic praise, and (contrast) genuine agreement. Each is encoded along a distinct linear direction in latent space, independently steerable via activation additions. Structure consistent across model families and scales. Establishes that sycophancy is plural, not single.

papers.title: AI safety via debate
papers.summary: Original debate proposal: two AI debaters in a zero-sum game judged by a (weaker) human. MNIST proof-of-concept: 6-pixel classifier accuracy 59.4% -> 88.9% with debate; 4-pixel 48.2% -> 85.2%. Foundational reference for the scalable-oversight 'decompose the claim' lineage.

papers.title: On Scalable Oversight with Weak LLMs Judging Strong LLMs
papers.authors: Kenton, Siegel, Kramar, Brown-Cohen, Albanie, Bulian, Agarwal, Lindner, Tang, Goodman, Shah
papers.summary: Generalizes Irving 2018 debate to LLM debaters and (weaker) LLM judges. Debate vs consultancy vs direct-QA across math/coding/logic/multimodal/QA. Debate beats consultancy across all tasks; debate vs direct-QA is task-dependent (best on info-asymmetry QA). When debaters choose positions voluntarily, judges resist incorrect answers more in debate. Stronger debaters -> better judge accuracy.

papers.title: Scalable AI Safety via Doubly-Efficient Debate
papers.summary: Theoretical + empirical case that optimizing debaters for persuasiveness drives truth-finding under specific conditions. 76-88% accuracy. Strongest current positive result for debate. Phase 2 follow-up needed for full mechanism + ICML proceedings citation.

papers.title: On the Reliability of Watermarks for Large Language Models
papers.authors: Kirchenbauer, Geiping, Wen, Shu, Saifullah, Kong, Fernando, Saha, Goldblum, Goldstein
papers.summary: Reliability follow-up to Kirchenbauer 2023. Tests robustness under human paraphrase, LLM paraphrase, embedded watermark spans. Headline: detectable after 800 tokens on average under strong human paraphrasing at FPR 1e-5. Establishes that watermarks degrade but do not vanish under attack.

papers.title: JailbreakBench: An Open Robustness Benchmark for Jailbreaking Large Language Models
papers.authors: Chao, Debenedetti, Robey, Andriushchenko, Croce, Sehwag, Dobriban, Flammarion, Pappas, Tramer, Hassani, Wong
papers.summary: 100 target behaviors aligned with OpenAI usage policies. Standardized threat model, system prompts, chat templates, scoring functions. Public leaderboard tracking attacks and defenses. Repository of 'jailbreak artifacts' (adversarial prompts). Co-canonical with HarmBench for jailbreak evaluation.

papers.title: The Crescendo Multi-Turn LLM Jailbreak Attack
papers.summary: Multi-turn gradual escalation jailbreak. 29-71% ASR improvement over single-turn baselines on AdvBench. Documents that single-turn evals systematically underestimate attack surface against multi-turn adversaries.

papers.title: Anthropic's Responsible Scaling Policy
papers.summary: ASL (AI Safety Level) tier framework: ASL-1 (low risk), ASL-2 (current frontier baseline), ASL-3 (substantially elevated risk; deployment controls), ASL-4 (autonomous AI risk). Each tier specifies capability evals that must trigger pre-deployment and security/containment commitments. Co-canonical with OpenAI Preparedness Framework and DeepMind Frontier Safety Framework.

papers.title: METR Autonomy Evaluation Resources
papers.summary: 77-task dangerous-capability suite for autonomous replication, research, and agent-level operation. Used by AISI for pre-deployment evals. METR task-horizon metric (human time-to-complete distribution) is the field's emerging lingua franca for capability quantification.

papers.title: Discovering Language Model Behaviors with Model-Written Evaluations
papers.summary: Anthropic discovering-LM-behaviors evals: model-written probes for sycophancy, power-seeking, self-preservation, etc. Earliest large-scale automated probe for sycophancy in production LLMs; precedes Sharma et al. 2023's deeper RLHF-mechanism analysis.

papers.title: SWE-bench: Can Language Models Resolve Real-World GitHub Issues?
papers.authors: Jimenez, Yang, Wettig, Yao, Pei, Press, Narasimhan
papers.summary: Introduces SWE-bench: 2,294 real GitHub issues across 12 Python repos. The FAIL_TO_PASS / PASS_TO_PASS regression-test verifier is the load-bearing methodological detail that made repo-level coding eval tractable. SWE-bench Verified (OpenAI Aug 2024) is the 500-task human-validated subset that became the de-facto leaderboard target.

papers.title: GAIA: A Benchmark for General AI Assistants
papers.authors: Mialon, Fourrier, Wolf, Lavril, LeCun, et al. (HuggingFace + Meta)
papers.summary: 466 tool-use questions (300 leaderboard / 166 dev) stratified into Levels 1/2/3. Headline gap: human 92% vs GPT-4 + plugins 15%. Gated-leaderboard methodology (gold answers held server-side) is the contamination-defense design point.

papers.title: OSWorld: Benchmarking Multimodal Agents for Open-Ended Tasks in Real Computer Environments
papers.authors: Xie, Zhang, Chen, Lou, Liu, Hu, Xing, Hu, Lin, Liu, Yu
papers.summary: 369 computer-use tasks across Ubuntu/Windows/macOS with per-task executable evaluator scripts checking OS end-state. Headline gap at launch: humans 72% vs GPT-4V 12%. Failure mode: GUI grounding bottleneck.

papers.title: tau-bench: A Benchmark for Tool-Agent-User Interaction in Real-World Domains
papers.authors: Yao, Zhao, Wang, Schick, Lewis, Yih, Lecun, Yu (Sierra Research)
papers.summary: Customer-service agentic benchmark with simulated-user + policy compliance + DB-end-state verifier. Introduces pass^k metric (all k attempts must succeed; the production-reliability dual of pass@k). GPT-4o: <50% pass@1, <25% pass^8 on retail.

papers.title: Holistic Evaluation of Language Models
papers.authors: Liang, Bommasani, Lee, Tsipras, Soylu, Yasunaga, Zhang, Narayanan, Wu, et al. (Stanford CRFM)
papers.summary: HELM: 7-metric x 16-scenario evaluation framework. The structural argument that benchmarks should ship metric bundles (accuracy, calibration, robustness, fairness, bias, toxicity, efficiency), not single metrics. Increased coverage from 17.9% to 96% of core scenarios across 30 LMs at launch.

papers.title: Benchmarking LLMs Under Data Contamination: From Static to Dynamic Evaluation
papers.summary: Comprehensive survey of contamination problem and mitigation strategies. Establishes the static-vs-dynamic-evaluation taxonomy. Identifies key open problem: lack of standardized criteria for evaluating dynamic benchmarks.

papers.title: EleutherAI Language Model Evaluation Harness
papers.authors: Gao, Tow, Abbasi, Biderman, Black, et al. (EleutherAI)
papers.summary: Open-source eval infrastructure backing HF Open LLM Leaderboard. 60+ benchmarks reduced to three request-type primitives (loglikelihood / loglikelihood_rolling / generate_until). Adopted by NVIDIA, Cohere, BigScience, Mosaic ML internally; the de-facto standard for open-model evaluation.

papers.title: Are We Done with MMLU?
papers.summary: Verifier-audit of MMLU. Re-annotates 5,700 questions across 57 subjects; finds 6.49% aggregate error rate; per-subset rates up to 57% (Virology). Documents leaderboard-rank flips when corrected.

papers.title: MathArena: Evaluating LLMs on Uncontaminated Math Competitions
papers.summary: Dynamic benchmark using post-cutoff competition problems (CMIMC 2025, IMO 2025, etc.) to defeat training-data contamination. Evaluates models on problems immediately upon release. >50 models, 7 competitions, 162 problems as of 2025.

papers.title: Cybench: A Framework for Evaluating Cybersecurity Capabilities and Risks of Language Models
papers.summary: 40 professional Capture-the-Flag cybersecurity tasks across 4 CTF competitions. Used by US AISI / UK AISI for pre-deployment dangerous-capability evals. Top frontier models (Claude 3.5 Sonnet, GPT-4o, o1-preview, Claude 3 Opus) solve tasks taking human teams up to 11 minutes.

papers.title: AgentBench: Evaluating LLMs as Agents
papers.summary: 8-environment agentic benchmark (OS, DB, KG, web, etc.). Headline finding: significant capability gap between top commercial LLMs and open-source <70B alternatives. Failure mode: poor long-term reasoning, decision-making, instruction following.

papers.title: ARC-AGI-2 (and ARC Prize 2025 Technical Report)
papers.authors: Chollet et al. (ARC Prize Foundation); Chapman et al. for tech report
papers.summary: Successor to ARC-AGI-1 (which was effectively solved by o3 at >85% in late 2024). ARC-AGI-2 launches early 2025; top commercial model ~37.6%, refinement-loop systems ~54%, human baseline ~95%. Tests inductive grid-puzzle generalization with minimal world knowledge.

papers.title: Efficient Memory Management for Large Language Model Serving with PagedAttention
papers.authors: Kwon, Li, Zhuang, Sheng, Zheng, Yu, Gonzalez, Zhang, Stoica
papers.summary: PagedAttention + vLLM. Treats the KV cache as virtual memory: fixed-size KV blocks, indirection via per-sequence block tables, copy-on-write for forking decode paths, and recompute/swap policies for eviction. Eq.4 expresses attention as a sum over KV blocks. Reframes serving from a contiguous-allocation problem (50-90% wasted memory in FasterTransformer/Orca per their measurements) to a paging problem with <4% internal fragmentation. The substrate every modern serving stack inherits.

papers.title: GPTQ: Accurate Post-Training Quantization for Generative Pre-trained Transformers
papers.summary: Layer-wise post-training weight quantization via OBQ-style second-order updates with three engineering modifications: arbitrary fixed quantization order (eliminates the per-row sort), lazy batched updates (amortises BLAS cost across blocks of 128 columns), and Cholesky reformulation (numerically stable for the Hessian inverse). Quantizes a 175B OPT/BLOOM in ~4 GPU-hours to 3-4 bit with negligible perplexity loss. The reference algorithm for W4A16 weight-only quantization.

papers.title: Fast Inference from Transformers via Speculative Decoding
papers.summary: The canonical speculative-decoding paper. A small drafter Mq proposes gamma tokens autoregressively; the target Mp scores them in one parallel forward pass; a rejection-sampling rule (accept if r <= p(x)/q(x), else resample from norm(max(0, p-q))) preserves the exact target distribution. Theorem 3.5 gives E[#accepted tokens] = (1-alpha^(gamma+1))/(1-alpha); Theorem 3.8 gives the walltime speedup as a function of alpha and the cost ratio c = T_q/T_p. T5-XXL 2-3x walltime improvement at c approx 0.05 and alpha approx 0.7.

papers.title: EAGLE: Speculative Sampling Requires Rethinking Feature Uncertainty
papers.summary: Drafts at the feature (penultimate-layer hidden state) level, not the token level. The drafter is a single transformer layer that autoregresses on a sequence of (feature_t, token_{t+1}) pairs - the shifted-token trick - because token_{t+1} disambiguates which sample produced feature_t. Trained with SmoothL1 (feature) + cross-entropy (token) losses. Tree-structured drafts via the same target-LM-driven verification that Medusa uses. Reports ~3x speedup on Vicuna and consistent gains across temperatures.

papers.title: Medusa: Simple LLM Inference Acceleration Framework with Multiple Decoding Heads
papers.authors: Cai, Li, Geng, Peng, Lee, Zhang, Chen, Dao
papers.summary: K parallel decoding heads on top of the frozen target model, each predicting position t+k+1 from the same hidden state. Drafts form a tree (Cartesian product of per-head top-k) verified in one forward pass via tree-attention masks. Medusa-1: heads-only training. Medusa-2: jointly fine-tunes the backbone with a head-loss + lambda * LM-loss schedule. Introduces 'typical acceptance' (accept any draft above an entropy-scaled threshold rather than exact-match sampling) as a quality-throughput knob. ~2-3x speedup, no separate drafter model.

papers.title: SmoothQuant: Accurate and Efficient Post-Training Quantization for Large Language Models
papers.authors: Xiao, Lin, Seznec, Wu, Demouth, Han
papers.summary: Identifies the activation-outlier problem above ~6.7B parameters: a small number of channels carry magnitudes 100x the median, breaking per-tensor INT8 activation quantization. Migrates outlier difficulty offline from activations to weights via diag(s) on the linear-layer input, with smoothing factor s_j = max(|X_j|)^alpha / max(|W_j|)^(1-alpha) and alpha=0.5 default (Eq.3-4). Enables W8A8 INT8 quantization on OPT/BLOOM/LLaMA up to 530B with ~1.56x speedup and within 0.1-0.2 perplexity of FP16.

papers.title: SGLang: Efficient Execution of Structured Language Model Programs
papers.authors: Zheng, Yin, Xie, Sun, Huang, Yu, Cao, Kozyrakis, Stoica, Gonzalez, Barrett, Sheng
papers.summary: Two contributions: (1) RadixAttention - cross-request KV reuse via a radix-tree of token-prefix keys with LRU + reference-count eviction and cache-aware DFS scheduling that maximises tree hit-rate (Theorem 3.1). (2) Compressed FSM for constrained decoding - merges single-edge runs of deterministic transitions so a forced prefix like '{"summary": "' is decoded in one step, not seven. The combination delivers 1.5-6x throughput over vLLM and TGI on shared-prefix and JSON-schema workloads.

papers.title: Accelerating Large Language Model Decoding with Speculative Sampling
papers.authors: Chen, Borgeaud, Irving, Lespiau, Sifre, Jumper
papers.summary: Concurrent (with leviathan2023) speculative-sampling formulation from DeepMind. Uses Chinchilla 70B as target with a 4B drafter; reports ~2-2.5x walltime speedup. Same rejection-sampling correctness guarantee as Leviathan. Cited as the second canonical reference for the technique; full excerpt deferred since the algorithmic content is captured by leviathan2023.

papers.title: Towards Best Practices of Activation Patching in Language Models: Metrics and Methods
papers.summary: First systematic methodology study of activation patching: shows the choice of corruption method (Gaussian Noising vs. Symmetric Token Replacement) and metric (probability vs. logit difference vs. KL) materially changes which components get implicated. Recommends STR + logit difference as default. The paper that turned activation patching from artisanal to documented protocol.

papers.title: How to Use and Interpret Activation Patching
papers.summary: Practitioner's guide to activation patching written by Anthropic and independent researchers. Pedagogical companion to Zhang & Nanda 2023. Not yet excerpted — Phase 2 follow-up.

papers.title: Towards Automated Circuit Discovery for Mechanistic Interpretability (ACDC)
papers.summary: Automated Circuit DisCovery: iteratively path-patches every edge in the model's computational graph, prunes edges with patching effect below threshold, returns the resulting subgraph. First fully-automated mech-interp circuit discovery method. Not yet excerpted — Phase 2 follow-up.

papers.title: Jumping Ahead: Improving Reconstruction Fidelity with JumpReLU Sparse Autoencoders
papers.authors: Rajamanoharan, Lieberum, Sonnerat, Conmy, Varma, Kramár, Nanda
papers.summary: JumpReLU SAE: replace SAE encoder ReLU with a discontinuous JumpReLU activation, train with L0 penalty (not L1), use straight-through estimators (STEs) to backprop through the discontinuity. SOTA reconstruction at fixed L0 on Gemma 2 9B; matches/beats TopK with cheaper training (no partial sort). The SAE architecture used in Gemma Scope.

papers.title: Scaling and evaluating sparse autoencoders
papers.authors: Gao, Dupré la Tour, Tillman, Goh, Troll, Radford, Sutskever, Leike, Wu
papers.summary: OpenAI TopK SAEs + scaling laws L(C) and L(N, K) for SAE training. Trains a 16M-latent SAE on GPT-4 activations for 40B tokens (~20% of GPT-3 pretraining compute). Establishes auxiliary AuxK loss for dead-latent prevention; ~7% dead latents in 16M-latent SAE.

papers.title: Transcoders Find Interpretable LLM Feature Circuits
papers.summary: Transcoders: a wide ReLU MLP that learns to mimic the input/output behavior of an MLP sublayer with sparse hidden activations. Unlike SAEs (which decode activation back to itself), transcoders give an *input-invariant* factorization of MLP computations into sparse feature circuits. Architectural primitive of Anthropic's 2025 attribution-graph framework.

papers.title: Sparse Autoencoders Do Not Find Canonical Units of Analysis
papers.authors: Leask, Bussmann, Pearce, Bloom, Tigges, Al Moubayed, Sharkey, Nanda
papers.summary: Negative result: SAE stitching shows larger SAEs find features absent from smaller ones (incompleteness); meta-SAEs show larger latents decompose into combinations of smaller latents (non-atomicity). Direct contradiction with the 'true features' framing of Bricken 2023 / Templeton 2024. Note: this entry supersedes the existing `sae-not-canonical-2025` placeholder in papers.json — same key, now has local_file + excerpts_file + correct authors. The arxiv ID for this paper is 2502.04878 (the URL in the existing papers.json entry was an ICLR PDF link, which is also valid).

papers.title: The Geometry of Truth: Emergent Linear Structure in Large Language Model Representations of True/False Datasets
papers.summary: Mass-mean probing for truth: the 'truth direction' is the difference between mean activations on true vs. false statements. Generalizes across logical transformations where logistic-regression probes fail. Note: Phase 1 sweep mis-attributed this to 'Burns et al.' — actual authors are Samuel Marks and Max Tegmark. The unrelated `burns2023-w2s` paper is the Burns et al. (OpenAI Superalignment) weak-to-strong generalization paper, in the alignment area.

papers.title: On the Biology of a Large Language Model
papers.summary: Application of circuit tracing to Claude 3.5 Haiku. Documents forward-planning in poetry (rhyme target features activate before the rhyme word), multi-hop reasoning chains, refusal circuits, language-routing in non-English contexts. HTML-only — Phase 2 follow-up needed.

papers.title: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
papers.authors: Ouyang, Wu, Jiang, Almeida, Wainwright, Mishkin, Zhang, Agarwal, Slama, Ray, Schulman, Hilton, Kelton, Miller, Simens, Askell, Welinder, Christiano, Leike, Lowe
papers.summary: InstructGPT: canonical 3-stage RLHF pipeline (SFT -> reward model -> PPO with KL anchor). 1.3B aligned model preferred over 175B GPT-3 on user prompts. The reference for everything that 'RLHF' means in 2022-2024.

papers.title: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences
papers.authors: Christiano, Leike, Brown, Martic, Legg, Amodei
papers.summary: Foundational preference-RL paper. Establishes that complex tasks (Atari, simulated locomotion) can be learned from <1% of pairwise preference comparisons rather than reward signals. Direct technical ancestor of Stiennon 2020 and InstructGPT.

papers.title: Learning to Summarize from Human Feedback
papers.authors: Stiennon, Ouyang, Wu, Ziegler, Lowe, Voss, Radford, Amodei, Christiano
papers.summary: Summarization with human-preference RL. Establishes the Bradley-Terry RM loss + per-token-KL PPO objective form that InstructGPT 2022 inherits and that all subsequent LM-RLHF papers derive from.

papers.title: Proximal Policy Optimization Algorithms
papers.summary: PPO: clipped surrogate objective for policy gradient. The underlying RL algorithm used by InstructGPT, GRPO, DAPO, VAPO, REINFORCE++.

papers.title: Training a Helpful and Harmless Assistant with Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback
papers.authors: Bai, Jones, Ndousse, Askell, Chen, DasSarma, Drain, Fort, Ganguli, Henighan, Joseph, Kadavath, Kernion, Conerly, El-Showk, Elhage, Hatfield-Dodds, Hernandez, Hume, Johnston, Kravec, Lovitt, Nanda, Olsson, Amodei, Brown, Clark, McCandlish, Olah, Mann, Kaplan
papers.summary: Anthropic's HH-RLHF paper. Training assistants jointly for helpfulness and harmlessness; introduces the HH preference dataset that became a standard reference.

papers.title: DeepSeekMath: Pushing the Limits of Mathematical Reasoning in Open Language Models
papers.summary: Origin of GRPO (Group Relative Policy Optimization): critic-free PPO variant using group-mean reward as value baseline. The algorithm that R1, Qwen3, Tulu 3 RLVR all use. Memory-efficient: removes the value model that PPO requires.

papers.title: A General Theoretical Paradigm to Understand Learning from Human Preferences (IPO)
papers.authors: Azar, Rowland, Piot, Guo, Calandriello, Valko, Munos
papers.summary: IPO: replaces DPO's log-sigmoid loss with squared loss on the preference gap. Addresses DPO's overconfidence pathology where the implicit reward gap can grow unboundedly while the loss vanishes.

papers.title: KTO: Model Alignment as Prospect Theoretic Optimization
papers.summary: KTO (Kahneman-Tversky Optimization): operates on single-rating data {(x,y,label)} with label in {good,bad}, not pairwise preferences. Asymmetric prospect-theoretic loss (loss aversion). Useful when only yes/no labels are available.

papers.title: ORPO: Monolithic Preference Optimization without Reference Model
papers.summary: ORPO: combines SFT and preference losses in a single objective (log-odds ratio of SFT loss between chosen/rejected). No reference model, no SFT stage - trains from base directly. Reduces compute and pipeline complexity.

papers.title: MAGPIE: Alignment Data Synthesis from Scratch by Prompting Aligned LLMs with Nothing
papers.authors: Xu, Jiang, Niu, Deng, Lin, Poovendran, Choi, Lin
papers.summary: Pre-query template trick: prompt aligned LLMs with only the instruction prefix (no question), have them fabricate the question first, then sample the answer. Near-zero-cost SFT data synthesis. 4M instruction set; beats UltraFeedback SFT+DPO.

papers.title: C3AI: Crafting and Evaluating Constitutions for Constitutional AI
papers.summary: Systematic study of constitution design choices: which principles matter, which transfer between models, which are model-specific. Begins automated constitution design.

papers.title: Reverse Constitutional AI: Controllable Toxic Data Generation
papers.summary: Reverse-CAI (April 2026): inverts the constitution for adversarial data synthesis. Probability-clamped RLAIF generates controlled toxic / harmful content for red-teaming.

papers.title: VAPO: Value Augmented Proximal Policy Optimization
papers.summary: VAPO: re-introduces a learned value function on top of GRPO-style RL, with shared backbone and value-loss-clipping. Reportedly outperforms DAPO on long-CoT tasks. Single-source result; cross-replication pending.

papers.title: VinePPO: Refining Credit Assignment in RL Training of LLMs
papers.summary: VinePPO: per-state Monte Carlo re-simulation as value estimate. 3x wall-clock speedup over PPO on MATH/GSM8K. Like applying GRPO's group-mean idea at every prefix instead of just the prompt.

papers.title: RewardBench 2: Advancing Reward Model Evaluation
papers.summary: RewardBench 2: 2025 benchmark suite for RM evaluation. Successor to original RewardBench. Open question (per Phase 1 sweep): does score correlate with downstream RLHF outcome?

papers.title: WARM: On the Benefits of Weight Averaged Reward Models
papers.authors: Ramé, Couairon, Shukor, Thom, Pendse, Adda, Vincent, Mary, Cord, Aubert, Cordier, Joulin, Jégou, Dehghani
papers.summary: WARM: weight-averaged reward models. Average parameters of K independently-trained RMs to reduce overoptimization and improve robustness. Adopted in Gemma 3 post-training.

papers.title: WARP: On the Benefits of Weight Averaged Rewarded Policies
papers.authors: Ramé, Ferret, Vieillard, Dadashi, Hussenot, Cedoz, Sessa, Pernias, Geist, Bachem
papers.summary: WARP: alternates RL training with weight averaging. Companion to WARM at the policy side. Used together in Gemma 3 post-training.

papers.title: Scaling Laws for Reward Model Overoptimization in Direct Alignment Algorithms
papers.summary: Overoptimization scaling curves for DPO-class methods. Shows DPO suffers from analogous proxy/gold-reward gap as PPO-RLHF. Contradicts early DPO narrative that 'no explicit reward model means no reward hacking.'

papers.title: Large Language Models are Zero-Shot Reasoners
papers.summary: Zero-shot CoT: appending 'Let's think step by step' elicits CoT-style reasoning without exemplars. text-davinci-002 MultiArith 17.7% -> 78.7%; GSM8K 10.4% -> 40.7%. Two-stage prompt: reasoning extraction + answer extraction.

papers.title: Self-Consistency Improves Chain of Thought Reasoning in Language Models
papers.authors: Wang, Wei, Schuurmans, Le, Chi, Narang, Chowdhery, Zhou
papers.summary: Self-consistency: sample N reasoning paths at T > 0, majority-vote over extracted answers. GSM8K +17.9%, SVAMP +11.0%, AQuA +12.2% over greedy CoT. The cheapest test-time-compute strategy and the baseline against which best-of-N + verifier methods are compared.

papers.title: Let's Verify Step by Step
papers.authors: Lightman, Kosaraju, Burda, Edwards, Baker, Lee, Leike, Schulman, Sutskever, Cobbe
papers.summary: Process Reward Models on PRM800K. Process supervision substantially outperforms outcome supervision on MATH best-of-N at matched N. min-aggregation over per-step PRM scores is the strongest aggregator. Released the PRM800K dataset (800K human step-labels).

papers.title: ReST-MCTS*: LLM Self-Training via Process Reward Guided Tree Search
papers.authors: Zhang, Zhoubian, Hu, Yue, Dong, Tang
papers.summary: Reinforced self-training that integrates MCTS with process-reward guidance. Inferred process rewards (from oracle final answer probability) are used both as PRM training targets and as trace-quality signals for policy self-training. Outperforms Best-of-N and Tree-of-Thought at matched search budget.

papers.title: Unifying Tree Search Algorithm and Reward Design for LLM Reasoning: A Survey
papers.summary: Survey unifying classical tree-search principles (BFS/DFS/A*/MCTS) with their modern application to LLM reasoning under the Test-Time Scaling framework. Covers MCTS-family methods (selection-expansion-simulation-backprop) and informed-search variants; relates search budget, reward design, and reasoning quality.

papers.title: FaithCoT-Bench: Benchmarking Instance-Level Faithfulness of Chain-of-Thought Reasoning
papers.summary: First instance-level CoT-faithfulness benchmark. Formulates unfaithfulness detection as a discriminative task and provides FINE-CoT, an expert-annotated set of >1000 trajectories from four LLMs across four domains (>300 unfaithful). Evaluates 11 detectors spanning counterfactual, logit-based, and LLM-as-judge paradigms; finds detection harder in knowledge-intensive domains and on more advanced models.

papers.title: Chain-of-Thought Reasoning In The Wild Is Not Always Faithful
papers.authors: Arcuschin, Janiak, Krzyzanowski, Rajamanoharan, Nanda, Conmy
papers.summary: Demonstrates unfaithful CoT on realistic prompts with no artificial bias: models often post-hoc-rationalize implicitly biased responses rather than letting reasoning lead. Reports per-model rationalization rates: GPT-4o-mini 13%, Haiku 3.5 7%; thinking models far better but never zero (Sonnet 3.7 thinking 0.04%, Gemini 2.5 Pro 0.14%, R1 0.37%). Also identifies Unfaithful Illogical Shortcuts on hard math problems.

papers.title: BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding
papers.summary: Encoder-only Transformer pre-trained with masked LM + next-sentence prediction. Establishes the encoder track parallel to the GPT decoder track and seeds the BERT-derivative line (RoBERTa, DeBERTa, etc.). Cited primarily here as a historical anchor for the bifurcation into encoder-only / decoder-only / encoder-decoder.

papers.title: Language Models are Unsupervised Multitask Learners
papers.authors: Radford, Wu, Child, Luan, Amodei, Sutskever
papers.summary: GPT-2: 1.5B-parameter decoder-only LM trained on WebText. First strong evidence of zero-shot task transfer from a generative LM at scale, motivating the scaling-laws era that followed.

papers.title: Math-Shepherd: Verify and Reinforce LLMs Step-by-step without Human Annotations
papers.authors: Wang, Li, Shao, Xu, Dai, Li, Chen, Wu, Sui
papers.summary: Process-reward training without human step labels: derives per-step rewards by Monte Carlo rollout completion rate. Enables PRM training at scale and becomes the baseline against which subsequent open-PRM work (EDU-PRM, R-PRM, ThinkPRM) is compared.

papers.title: R-PRM: Reasoning-Driven Process Reward Modeling
papers.authors: She, Liu, Liu, Chen, Huang, Huang
papers.summary: PRM that emits a reasoning trace before scoring each step rather than scoring directly. Bootstraps from limited annotations via stronger-LLM seed data, refines via preference optimization, and applies inference-time scaling at scoring time. +11.9 / +8.5 F1 on ProcessBench / PRMBench over baselines; +8.5 acc on six math-reasoning suites when used as guide.

papers.title: More Bang for the Buck: Process Reward Modeling with Entropy-Driven Uncertainty
papers.summary: Anchors PRM step boundaries at high-predictive-entropy tokens rather than fixed/manual partitioning. Trains on 1.5% of usual data and beats Math-Shepherd / Omega-PRM on ProcessBench; EDU sampling raises generative-reasoning acc 64.7%->67.3% with -32% tokens.

papers.title: A Survey of Process Reward Models: From Outcome Signals to Process Supervisions for Large Language Models
papers.summary: Systematic survey of PRMs across the full loop: process-data generation, PRM training, and downstream use for test-time scaling and RL. Taxonomizes generative-vs-discriminative PRMs and tracks the field's progression from outcome-only signals (ORMs) to step-level process supervision.

papers.title: MCTS-RAG: Enhancing Retrieval-Augmented Generation with Monte Carlo Tree Search
papers.summary: Integrates MCTS with RAG: search expands over reasoning + retrieval decisions jointly rather than treating retrieval as a static prefix. Lets small LMs match GPT-4o-class performance on ComplexWebQA / GPQA / FoolMeTwice by scaling inference-time compute over a hybrid search-retrieve tree.

papers.title: Does Inference Scaling Improve Reasoning Faithfulness? A Multi-Model Analysis of Self-Consistency Tradeoffs
papers.summary: Empirical study of self-consistency tradeoffs across four frontier models (GPT-5.2, Claude Opus 4.5, Gemini-3-flash-preview, DeepSeek-v3.2) on 100 GSM8K problems. Finds inference scaling does not uniformly improve faithfulness: e.g., Claude Opus 4.5 accuracy 78% -> 74.3% while measured faithfulness rises 0.270 -> 0.891 at N=5. Self-consistency must be tested per-model before deployment.

papers.title: DoLa: Decoding by Contrasting Layers Improves Factuality in Large Language Models
papers.authors: Chuang, Xie, Luo, Kim, Glass, He
papers.summary: Decoding-time application of the logit-lens framework: contrast logits at the final layer against logits at an adaptively-chosen earlier layer (chosen per-token by JS divergence) to amplify factual signal and suppress hallucination. Improves TruthfulQA and FACTOR scores on LLaMA-7B/13B/33B with negligible inference overhead. Note: PDF not yet downloaded; excerpts from arXiv abstract pending grounding via WebSearch.

papers.title: Understanding intermediate layers using linear classifier probes
papers.summary: Foundational paper for the linear-classifier-probe methodology. Introduces the probe definition: a linear classifier trained on frozen network activations to predict a property, with the network's parameters held fixed. Empirical finding: linear separability of features increases monotonically with depth on Inception v3 / ResNet-50. The methodology generalizes to NLP probing in subsequent work. PDF not yet downloaded.

papers.title: A Structural Probe for Finding Syntax in Word Representations
papers.summary: Generalizes linear probing from binary classification to geometric / structural probing of higher-arity properties. Defines a 'structural probe' that learns a linear transform B such that squared-L2 distance in B-space approximates parse-tree distance, and a depth probe such that squared-L2 norm approximates parse-tree depth. Finds such transforms exist for ELMo and BERT but not for randomly-initialized baselines. PDF not yet downloaded.

papers.title: Probing Classifiers: Promises, Shortcomings, and Advances
papers.summary: Methodology survey that consolidates the correlational critique of probing and reviews causal alternatives. Three confounders documented: probe expressivity (powerful probe learns property from low-information features), spurious correlation (probe reads correlated feature, not target), vestigial information (model represents but doesn't use). Surveys causal probing methods (Amnesic, causal mediation, interchange interventions). PDF not yet downloaded.

papers.title: Jailbroken: How Does LLM Safety Training Fail?
papers.summary: Articulates the two-failure-mode framework for jailbreaks: (1) competing objectives (capability vs. safety pull in different directions), (2) mismatched generalization (safety training fails to generalize to domains where capability exists, e.g., Base64 / role-play). Empirically demonstrates that attacks designed against either failure mode succeed on every prompt in red-team evaluation sets for GPT-4 and Claude v1.3. Recommends 'safety-capability parity' as the policy upshot. PDF not yet downloaded.

papers.title: interpreting GPT: the logit lens
papers.summary: The original LessWrong post introducing the logit lens technique on GPT-2. Tier B/C forum source — cited as the discovery-path provenance for the technique, never as the sole citation for hard claims. The peer-reviewed treatment of the underlying methodology is `belrose2023-tuned-lens`.

papers.title: Mixed Precision Training
papers.authors: Micikevicius, Narang, Alben, Diamos, Elsen, Garcia, Ginsburg, Houston, Kuchaiev, Venkatesh, Wu
papers.summary: Canonical mixed-precision recipe: FP16 forward/backward + FP32 master weights + dynamic loss scaling + FP32 GEMM accumulators. The substrate that all modern lower-precision training extends.

papers.title: A Study of BFLOAT16 for Deep Learning Training
papers.summary: Establishes BF16 (8-bit exponent, 7-bit mantissa) as a drop-in FP32 replacement for deep-learning training, eliminating the need for loss scaling and (often) FP32 master weights. The universal LLM pre-training default by 2023.

papers.title: Megatron-LM: Training Multi-Billion Parameter Language Models Using Model Parallelism
papers.authors: Shoeybi, Patwary, Puri, LeGresley, Casper, Catanzaro
papers.summary: Original Megatron-LM tensor-parallelism paper. Cited here for the mixed-precision communication-precision discussion.

papers.title: LoRA: Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Language Models
papers.authors: Hu, Shen, Wallis, Allen-Zhu, Li, Wang, Wang, Chen
papers.summary: Canonical low-rank parameter-efficient fine-tuning. Inject trainable B*A rank-r decomposition alongside frozen base weights; merge for inference.

papers.title: QLoRA: Efficient Finetuning of Quantized LLMs
papers.summary: LoRA over a 4-bit-quantized (NF4) base, with double quantization and paged optimizers. Lets a 65B parameter model fine-tune on a single 48GB GPU.

papers.title: Model soups: averaging weights of multiple fine-tuned models improves accuracy without increasing inference time
papers.authors: Wortsman, Ilharco, Gadre, Roelofs, Gontijo-Lopes, Morcos, Namkoong, Farhadi, Carmon, Kornblith, Schmidt
papers.summary: Averaging weights of multiple fine-tunes from a shared base produces a single model that often beats the best individual fine-tune. Foundational to the merging literature; relies on linear mode connectivity.

papers.title: Editing Models with Task Arithmetic
papers.authors: Ilharco, Ribeiro, Wortsman, Gururangan, Schmidt, Hajishirzi, Farhadi
papers.summary: Defines task vectors as fine-tune-minus-base directions in weight space; addition / negation / combination as primitives. Direct ancestor of TIES-Merging.

papers.title: TIES-Merging: Resolving Interference When Merging Models
papers.summary: Three-step model merge (Trim, Elect-Sign, Disjoint-Merge) addressing the sign-conflict and redundancy issues in naive task-vector averaging. The current default merge method in MergeKit.

papers.title: Efficient Guided Generation for Large Language Models
papers.summary: Reformulates constrained decoding as transitions in an FSM whose alphabet is the model's vocabulary. Builds a per-state index over allowed tokens, dropping per-step overhead from O(V) to amortized O(1). Foundation for Outlines, XGrammar, llguidance.

papers.title: Prompting Is Programming: A Query Language for Large Language Models
papers.summary: Introduces LMQL, a query language for LLMs that exposes constraints (regex, length, type) close to the prompt and translates them to per-step logit masks. The DSL-first approach to constrained decoding; predates the FSM-index optimization.

papers.title: Finetuned Language Models Are Zero-Shot Learners (FLAN)
papers.authors: Wei, Bosma, Zhao, Guu, Yu, Lester, Du, Dai, Le
papers.summary: Original instruction-tuning paper. Reformats ~1.8K NLP tasks as natural-language instructions; SFT on the multi-task collection produces strong zero-shot generalization. The pre-RLHF post-training default.

papers.title: Scaling Instruction-Finetuned Language Models (Flan-T5)
papers.authors: Chung, Hou, Longpre, Zoph, Tay, Fedus, Li, Wang, Dehghani, Brahma, Webson, Gu, Dai, Suzgun, Chen, Chowdhery, Castro-Ros, Pellat, Robinson, Valter, Narang, Mishra, Yu, Zhao, Huang, Dai, Yu, Petrov, Chi, Dean, Devlin, Roberts, Zhou, Le, Wei
papers.summary: Scales FLAN to 1.8K tasks with multiple prompting techniques (zero-shot, few-shot, CoT). Establishes that adding CoT examples to instruction-tuning data improves reasoning generalization.

papers.title: Flamingo: a Visual Language Model for Few-Shot Learning
papers.authors: Alayrac, Donahue, Luc, Miech, Barr, Hasson, Lenc, Mensch, Millican, Reynolds, Ring, Rutherford, Cabi, Han, Gong, Samangooei, Monteiro, Menick, Borgeaud, Brock, Nematzadeh, Sharifzadeh, Binkowski, Barreira, Vinyals, Zisserman, Simonyan
papers.summary: Flamingo: bridge a frozen vision encoder to a frozen LM via Perceiver Resampler + interleaved gated cross-attention layers; trained on interleaved image-text web data. Establishes the cross-attention-bridge architectural family for multimodal LLMs.

papers.title: Layer Normalization
papers.summary: LayerNorm: per-token normalization across the hidden dimension with learned gain and bias. The original normalization the canonical Transformer used; later largely superseded in LLMs by RMSNorm but the conceptual baseline.

papers.title: Better & Faster Large Language Models via Multi-token Prediction
papers.summary: Trains an LM with N parallel output heads predicting the next 1..N tokens jointly. Improves sample efficiency at scale and provides a built-in self-speculative-decoding hook. Distinct from speculative decoding (which uses a separate draft model).

papers.title: SentencePiece: A simple and language independent subword tokenizer and detokenizer for Neural Text Processing
papers.summary: Whitespace-agnostic, byte-level-capable subword tokenizer + detokenizer. Treats raw text as a Unicode byte stream and learns BPE or unigram-LM subwords on it. Becomes the standard tokenizer scaffolding for multilingual LMs.

papers.title: Using the Output Embedding to Improve Language Models
papers.summary: Tied weight scheme: share the input token embedding matrix with the output unembedding. Cuts ~30% of parameters in small LMs and improves PPL by serving as an implicit regularizer. The default in many decoder-only LMs through ~2023; modern frontier LMs sometimes untie.

papers.title: Linear Mode Connectivity and the Lottery Ticket Hypothesis
papers.summary: Empirical demonstration that fine-tunes of a shared base land in a connected loss basin: averaging weights along the line segment between two endpoints gives a model with loss similar to the endpoints. The leading explanation for why model merging works at all.

papers.title: Parameter-Efficient Transfer Learning for NLP
papers.authors: Houlsby, Giurgiu, Jastrzebski, Morrone, de Laroussilhe, Gesmundo, Attariyan, Gelly
papers.summary: Original adapter-tuning paper. Inserts small bottleneck modules between Transformer layers and trains only those, freezing the base. Establishes the parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) family that LoRA / QLoRA later refined.

papers.title: Train Short, Test Long: Attention with Linear Biases Enables Input Length Extrapolation
papers.summary: ALiBi: replaces positional embeddings with a linear bias subtracted from each head's attention scores as a function of distance. Enables training on short sequences and inference on much longer sequences without retraining. A late competitor to RoPE; lost on production scale but the extrapolation argument shaped subsequent long-context work.

papers.title: DeepNet: Scaling Transformers to 1,000 Layers
papers.authors: Wang, Ma, Dong, Huang, Zhang, Wei
papers.summary: DeepNorm: a normalization scheme that scales the residual branch by depth-dependent constants alpha and beta. Stabilizes training of 1000-layer Transformers. Bridges Pre-LN (which works at moderate depth but loses representational capacity) and Post-LN (which has the capacity but instability).

papers.title: Overthinking the Truth: Understanding how Language Models Process False Demonstrations
papers.summary: Documents the layer-of-prediction vs layer-of-decision phenomenon: in some prompts (false demonstrations), early-to-mid layer logit-lens predictions are more robust than the final-layer output. Establishes that 'the right layer to read out' has no a-priori recipe; cross-method evidence at the layer level is needed.

papers.title: Designing and Interpreting Probes with Control Tasks
papers.summary: Foundational methodology paper for probing. Introduces 'control tasks' (random labels) as a baseline; argues probe selectivity (gap between real-task and control-task accuracy) is a more honest measure than raw accuracy. Establishes that high-accuracy probes can be reading their own classifier capacity rather than the model's representations.

papers.title: Can AI-Generated Text be Reliably Detected?
papers.summary: Argues that under recursive paraphrasing, even watermark- and statistical-detector-based AI-text detectors converge toward random performance. Establishes the paraphrase-attack vector as a fundamental robustness limit on generation-time watermarking. Often cited as the 'cannot be reliably detected' counter-claim to the watermark-feasibility line.
